results.name: Almonds Sugar Coated
results.comments: Since the sugar coating is subordinate to the almond, even if the amount of sugar is greater than the volume of the almond (Eliyahu Raba 204:17; P’ri Megadim, Mechaber 202:13).

results.name: Apple Sauce
results.comments: Shehakol is to be said if the apples are totally pureed. This applies mostly to commercially available canned or bottled apple sauce. However, Ha-aytz applies for homemade apple sauce where the applesauce is chunky.

results.name: Apple Strudel
results.comments: As with all cakes, the dough is considered the primary ingredient and only Mezonot is necessary (Mishnah Berurah 212:1), even if there is less dough than apple, and even if the cake is eaten mainly because of the apple. To say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot. On the other hand, if there is merely a thin strip of dough, which is intended just to help you handle all the apple, the dough becomes subordinate to the apple and only Ha-aytz need be said.

results.name: Banana Split - For the banana
results.comments: Both brachot must be said because neither part of the food is subordinate to the other.

results.name: Banana Split - For the ice cream
results.comments: Both brachot must be said because neither part of the food is subordinate to the other.

results.name: Barley Soup
results.comments: To say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of barley within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.

results.name: Blackberry
results.comments: (See Igrot Moshe, Orach Chaim 85). See OU Guide for Checking Fruits and Vegetables page 45.

results.name: Blueberry
results.comments: See OU Guide for Checking Fruits and Vegetables page 46.

results.name: Brandy
results.comments: Even though it is made from wine, since it is distilled, it becomes Shehakol.

results.name: Buckwheat (Kashe)
results.comments: Buckwheat is not a kind of wheat, nor is it even one of the Five Principal Species.  It is a legume.  Therefore, neither Mezonot nor Hamotzi can be said over a product of buckwheat unless one of the Five Principal Species, such as wheat flour, even only in part, has been added to it.  Thus, if you eat buckwheat alone, only Ha-adamah is said (see Igrot Moshe, Orach Chaim 65).

results.name: Buckwheat Muffins (with Flour of Five Principal Species)
results.comments: Al Hamichya if k\'zayit of Five Principal Species flour eaten within a kdei achillat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise one says Borei Nefashot.

results.name: Buckwheat Muffins (without Flour of Five Principal Species)
results.comments: Shehakol because buckwheat no longer has its original form.

results.name: Buckwheat Pancakes (with Flour of Five Principal Species)
results.comments: Al Hamichya if k\'zayit of Five Principal Species flour eaten within a kdei achillat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise one says Borei Nefashot.

results.name: Buckwheat Pancakes (without Flour of Five Principal Species)
results.comments: Shehakol because buckwheat no longer has its original form.

results.name: Candy (hard)
results.comments: Borei Nefashot is only said if one eats a k’zayit within a kdei achillat pras (2-9 minutes) which usually is not the case.

results.name: Canyon Ancient Grain
results.comments: Hamotzi - netilas Yadayim without a beracha.

results.name: Canyon Bakehouse Sourdough Whole Grain Bread
Canyon Country White
results.comments: Hamotzi - netilas Yadayim without a beracha

results.name: Cheese Cake
results.comments: As with all cakes, the dough is considered the primary ingredient and only Mezonot is necessary (Mishnah Berurah 212:1), even if there is less dough than cheese, and even if the cake is eaten mainly because of the cheese. However, to say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.  On the other hand, if there is merely a thin strip of dough, which is intended just to help you handle all the cheese, the dough becomes subordinate to the cheese and only Shehakol need be said.

results.name: Cherry Pie or Cake
results.comments: To say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes).

results.name: Chicken Soup (with kneidlach or noodles)
results.comments: Al Hamichya on the kneidlach and Borei Nefashot on the soup

results.name: Chocolate Cream Pie
results.comments: Provided the flour is made of any of the Five Principal Species. Al Hamichya is only said if a k’zayit of flour was consumed within a kdei achillat pras (2-9 minutes).

results.name: Cinnamon
results.comments: (See Sefer Halacha 1:29a)

results.name: Coconut Cream Pie
results.comments: Provided the flour is made of any of the Five Principal Species. Al Hamichya is only said if a k’zayit of flour was consumed within a kdei achillat pras (2-9 minutes).

results.name: Cognac
results.comments: Even though it is made from wine, since it is distilled, it becomes Shehakol.

results.name: Corn Grits (cooked)
results.comments: Shehakol because the corn disintegrates and loses its shape. Does not contain wheat flour.

results.name: Corn on the Cob
results.comments: Indian corn, or maize (i.e., the type that grows on the cob) is not one of the Five Principal Species.

results.name: Cranberry
results.comments: They grow from small bushes in a bog.

results.name: Cream with Fruit
results.comments: If the fruit is the main part - Ha-eitz .  If the cream is the main part - Shehakol.

results.name: Custard Pie
results.comments: Provided the flour is made of any of the Five Principal Species. Al Hamichya is only said if a k’zayit of flour was consumed within a kdei achillat pras (2-9 minutes).

results.name: Derma (Stuffed Kishke)
results.comments: Since the ingredients include flour made from one or more of the Five Principal Species.

results.name: Etrog
results.comments: If prepared in a tasty manner, since otherwise it is not good for consumption.

results.name: Fruit Soup (if made with fruit requiring Ha-adamah )
Fruit Soup (If made with fruit requiring Ha-aytz )
Garlic
results.comments: If eaten raw or boiled- Shehakol. Fried in oil - Ha-adamah  (Mishnah Berurah 205:7; Igrot Moshe, Orach Chaim I:64).

results.name: Gefilte Fish
results.comments: Although gefilte fish may be prepared with bread crumbs or matza flour, their purpose is just to hold the ground fish and other ingredients together.  Thus, only Shehakol need be said.  (See also Shulchan Aruch, Orach Chaim 208b, and Mishnah Berurah 208:8).

results.name: Ginger, if raw or dry and candied
Granola Bar
results.comments: Shulchan Aruch writes that there is stringency to try avoid eating a k’zayit of the five grains when they are ha-adamah, but if one did they say Borei Nefashot.

results.name: Gum
results.comments: There is no beracha acharona on gum; One will not eat a kezayis of gum fast enough to recite borei Nifashos.

results.name: Ice Cream
results.comments: Even if ice cream is eaten in a cone, only Shehakol is necessary, since the cone is eaten only because of the ice cream and is clearly subordinate to it.  Its purpose is actually not so much to be eaten as to hold the ice cream and to prevent the hands from becoming soiled (see Shulchan Aruch, Orach Chaim 212b; Mishnah Berurah 212:5; Kaf Hachaim 15).  However, if you specifically use a sugar cone because you enjoy eating it, Mezonot must also be said before eating the cone.

results.name: Ice Cream
results.comments: Even if ice cream is eaten on a cone, only Shehakol is necessary, since the cone is eaten only because of the ice cream and is clearly subordinate to it.  Its purpose is actually not so much to be eaten as to hold the ice cream and to prevent the hands from becoming soiled (see Shulchan Aruch, Orach Chaim 212b; Mishnah Berurah 212:5, Kaf Hachaim 15).  However, if you specifically use a sugar cone because you enjoy eating it, Mezonot must also be said before eating the cone.

results.name: Kishke
results.comments: Provided the ingredients include flour made of any of the Five Principal Species.

results.name: Knish with Meat
results.comments: Provided the ingredients include flour made with one of the Five Principal Species. However, to say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of dough within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes).

results.name: Kugel - made from potatoes or vegetables
results.comments: As per Rav Belsky bracha rishona is ha-adamah even if made from potato flour, since today most potatoes are processed into flakes and flour. (See Mishnah Berurah 202:42-44)

results.name: Kugel Made-  from chalah or lokshen
Licorice Root (natural)
results.comments: Shehakol should be said (see Chayei Adam 51:15; Mishnah Berurah 202:76).

results.name: Macaroon
results.comments: Unless made with flour of one of the Five Principal Species, in which case Mezonot is required.

results.name: Matza Brei
results.comments: Provided that none of the pieces of matza is as large as a k\'zayit (i.e., about one-third of a matza).  If even one piece is that large, then Hamotzi is required.

results.name: Mushroom and Barley Soup
results.comments: To say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of barley within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.

results.name: Noodles for Pesach
results.comments: Since they do not contain flour made from the Five Principal Species.

results.name: Onion, raw
results.comments: Red onions which are normally eaten raw - Ha-adamah. If the onion is normally fried to be eaten - Shehakol

results.name: Orange Peel (even if candied)
results.comments: (See Mishna Berura 202:39, Chazon Ish, Orach Chaim 33b)

results.name: Papaya
results.comments: Some make Ha-adamah

results.name: Parsley
results.comments: It is unusual to eat parsley raw, therefore it is Shehakol. Cooked parsley would be Ha-adamah.

results.name: Peach Pie
results.comments: As with all cakes, the dough is considered the primary ingredient and only Mezonot is necessary (Mishnah Berurah 212:1), even if there is less dough than peaches, and even if the cake is eaten mainly because of the peaches. However, to say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.  On the other hand, if there is merely a thin strip of dough, which is intended just to help you handle all the peaches, the dough becomes subordinate to the peaches and only Ha-aytz need be said.

results.name: Peel (of Fruits)
results.comments: If part of fruit is attached to peel Ha-aytz is made.

results.name: Pie
results.comments: As with all cakes, the dough is considered the primary ingredient and only Mezonot is necessary (Mishnah Berurah 212:1), even if there is less dough than pie filling, and even if the cake is eaten mainly because of the pie filling. However, to say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.  On the other hand, if there is merely a thin strip of dough, which is intended just to help you handle all the pie filling, the dough becomes subordinate to the filling and only Ha-aytz need be said.

results.name: Pie
results.comments: As with all cakes, the dough is considered the primary ingredient and only Mezonot is necessary (Mishnah Berurah 212:1), even if there is less dough than pie filling, and even if the cake is eaten mainly because of the pie filling. However, to say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.  On the other hand, if there is merely a thin strip of dough, which is intended just to help you handle all the pie filling, the dough becomes subordinate to the filling and only Ha-aytz need be said.

results.name: Pizza (Meal)
results.comments: It is Pat Haba'ah B'kisnin. If two slices are eaten without other foods this would constitute a Keviat Seudah for an obligation of Birkat Hamazon. If pizza is eaten with other foods, such as french fries and salad, whatever amount of bread a person would usually eat at a similar meal with other foods would constitute Keviat Seudah of pizza even if less than two slices are eaten.

results.name: Plum Pie
results.comments: As with all cakes, the dough is considered the primary ingredient and only Mezonot is necessary (Mishnah Berurah 212:1), even if there is less dough than plums, and even if the cake is eaten mainly because of the plums. However, to say an Al Hamichya one must eat a k\'zayit of cake within a kdei achilat pras (2-9 minutes). Otherwise, one says a Borei Nefashot.  On the other hand, if there is merely a thin strip of dough, which is intended just to help you handle all the plums, the dough becomes subordinate to the plums and only Ha-aytz need be said.

results.name: Popcorn
results.comments: See Rav Moshe Feinstein, Sefer Torah V'hora'ah 12:13.

results.name: Potato Blintzes
results.comments: Provided the ingredients include flour made with one or more of the Five Principal Species.

results.name: Potato Knish
results.comments: Provided the ingredients include flour made with one or more of the Five Principal Species.

results.name: Potato Kugel (made from potato flour)
results.comments: As per Rav Belsky, since today most potatoes are processed into flakes and flour. (See Mishnah Berurah 202:42-44)

results.name: Potato Latkes (from potato flour)
results.comments: As per Rav Belsky, since today most potatoes are processed into flakes and flour. (See Mishnah Berurah 202:42-44)

results.name: Potato Latkes (from real potatoes)
results.comments: If a substantial amount of flour of the Five Principal Species is used, Mezonot is required.

results.name: Potatoes, Instant
results.comments: As per Rav Belsky, since today most potatoes are processed into flakes and flour. (See Mishnah Berurah 202:42-44)

results.name: Pringles
results.comments: As per Rav Belsky, since today most potatoes are processed into flakes and flour. (See Mishnah Berurah 202:42-44)

results.name: Prune Juice
results.comments: Since most plums are no longer grown for the purpose of making juice the brocho is shehakol.

results.name: Puffed Wheat
results.comments: Igerot Moshe O.C. IV:45  says that either Ha-adamah or Mezonot may be recited.

results.name: Raspberry
results.comments: See OU Guide for Checking Fruits and Vegetables page 47.

results.name: Rice Cakes
results.comments: Rav Belsky held that either Mezonos or Ha\'odama can be said. But he favored Mezonos.

results.name: RX Date Bars
results.comments: If one eats two bars within the time span of achilas pras (3-4 minutes, according to some up to 7 minutes) then one would recite Al ha’eitz and not Borei nifashos.

results.name: Soft Pretzels
results.comments: Meets all criteria of bread.

results.name: Spices
results.comments: No bracha if inedible

results.name: Strawberry (both wild and cultivated)
results.comments: (See Igrot Moshe, Orach Chaim 86). See OU Guide for Checking Fruits and Vegetables page 48.

results.name: Tomato Rice Soup
results.comments: If the main intention is the rice then one would say Mezonot, if one\'s main intention is the tomato, one would say Ha-adamah.

results.name: Tortilla
results.comments: Since it is made of maize flour.

results.name: Welsh Rarebit
results.comments: By itself. When eaten together with toast - Hamotzi is said followed by Birkat Hamazon.

results.name: Wraps
results.comments: If one were to eat a plain wrap (unfilled) then it would be mezonos. Because it is not the way people eat this, this is a chisaron in the tzuras hapas. But if one were to eat a filled wrap with tuna or cheese etc then the proper beracha would be hamotzi.
